mission.title: Install and Configure KubeVela on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeVela is a modern application delivery platform that simplifies deploying and operating applications across hybrid and multi-cloud environments. Installing KubeVela allows you to manage your applications more efficiently using the Open Application Model (OAM).

steps.title: Add the KubeVela Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official KubeVela Helm repository to your Helm configuration:

```bash
helm repo add kubevela https://kubevela.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KubeVela (vela-core)
steps.description:
Install the KubeVela core (vela-core) chart in the vela-system namespace:

```bash
helm install --create-namespace -n vela-system kubevela kubevela/vela-core --wait
```

The --wait flag ensures the command waits until all pods are ready.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the KubeVela controller and other components are running:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n vela-system
```

You should see the vela-core controller pod in Running state.

steps.title: Install the VelaUX dashboard (optional)
steps.description:
Optionally install the VelaUX dashboard addon for a web UI:

```bash
vela addon enable velaux
```

Or via Helm:

```bash
helm install --create-namespace -n vela-system velaux kubevela/velaux
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the KubeVela release:

```bash
helm uninstall kubevela -n vela-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove the OAM CRDs created by KubeVela:

```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep oam.dev | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the vela-system namespace:

```bash
kubectl delete namespace vela-system
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeVela
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repo and upgrade:

```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kubevela kubevela/vela-core -n vela-system --wait
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded components are running:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n vela-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Check the vela-core controller logs for errors:

```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vela-core -n vela-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'release already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
If the release already exists, use helm upgrade instead:

```bash
helm upgrade kubevela kubevela/vela-core -n vela-system --wait
```

troubleshooting.title: KubeVela controller not responding
troubleshooting.description:
Check the deployment status and events:

```bash
kubectl get deployment -n vela-system
kubectl describe deployment -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vela-core -n vela-system
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeVela will show the vela-core controller pod running in the vela-system namespace. You can then define Applications using the OAM model and deploy them across clusters.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster (v1.20+) running and Helm v3 and kubectl installed.
